jobs.purpose: Dispatched on Refund.status transition to `approved`. Calls the provider's createRefund method via `payment::PaymentProviderManager.instance()->createRefund()`. Persists provider_reference_id + provider_fee_reversal_cents on the refund row. Transitions status to `processing` on 2xx + async response; `succeeded` on immediate 2xx sync response (rare — most providers are async); `provider_error` on transient failure; `failed` on non-recoverable rejection. Fires RefundNotifiedProvider on successful dispatch.
steps: Load Refund row + parent Payment (with provider + provider_reference_id)
Refuse when refund.refunded_to != 'original_payment_method' — non-provider paths skip this job entirely (status transitions directly via admin flow)
Resolve provider driver: paymentProviderManager.instance(payment.provider_instance_name)
Call driver->createRefund(payment.provider_reference_id, refund.amount_cents, refund.reason)
On 2xx sync success: UPDATE refunds SET status='succeeded', provider_reference_id=?, provider_fee_reversal_cents=?, processed_at=NOW() — triggers RefundSucceeded finalization chain
On 2xx async processing: UPDATE refunds SET status='processing', provider_reference_id=?, provider_fee_reversal_cents=? — fires RefundProcessing + RefundNotifiedProvider
On 4xx non-recoverable: UPDATE refunds SET status='failed', failure_reason=?, failed_at=NOW() — fires RefundFailed
On 5xx transient or network error: UPDATE refunds SET status='provider_error', failure_reason=?, retry_count += 1 — fires RefundProviderError; ReconcileRefundsJob will retry
Emit stackra.refund.provider_dispatch_duration_ms histogram

jobs.purpose: Polls the provider periodically for async refunds. Some providers (Stripe, Adyen) take 5-10 days to fully process card refunds — this job checks the provider's authoritative status + reconciles our record. Per-provider cadence configurable (Stripe: hourly; Paddle: 4h; Adyen: hourly; Square: 4h; PayPal: hourly; CustomWebhook: caller-configured). Dispatched by ReconcileRefundsJob for every refund still in status=processing + provider_error.
steps: SELECT id, provider, provider_reference_id FROM refunds WHERE status IN ('processing', 'provider_error') AND deleted_at IS NULL FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 200
For each: resolve provider driver + call driver->getRefund(provider_reference_id)
Compare provider-native status to our local status
If provider succeeded: UPDATE refunds SET status='succeeded' + fire RefundSucceeded finalization chain + fire RefundProviderReconciled with drift_detected=true
If provider failed (non-recoverable): UPDATE refunds SET status='failed' + fire RefundFailed
If provider still processing: UPDATE refunds SET updated_at=NOW() (touch — signals we polled), fire RefundProviderReconciled with drift_detected=false
If provider returns 404 (refund reference not found — investigate ops): UPDATE refunds SET status='provider_error', failure_reason='provider_reference_not_found'
Emit stackra.refund.polls_total counter labelled by provider + terminal_state

jobs.purpose: Dispatched by RefundObserver.updating when status transitions to `succeeded`. Runs the finalization chain: creates offsetting Transaction via TransactionRecorder + creates CreditNote via CreditNoteIssuer + updates parent Invoice state + fires clawback events (RefundClawbackReferral + RefundClawbackCoupon) + fires marketing::RefundIssued negative-value conversion event. All five steps run in the same DB transaction — a Refund row NEVER exists in status=succeeded without its transaction record + credit note + invoice state update. On failure, the whole chain rolls back (transient) OR transitions the refund to `provider_error` for manual investigation (persistent).
steps: Open DB transaction
Step 1: transaction::TransactionRecorder::recordOffset($refund) — double-entry ledger (debit refunds_owed, credit cash). Persists transaction_id on refund row.
Step 2: invoice::CreditNoteIssuer::createFromRefund($refund) — creates reversal credit note. Persists credit_note_id on refund row.
Step 3: invoice::InvoiceStateUpdater::applyRefund($refund) — updates parent invoice status (refunded / partially_refunded) + accumulates amount_refunded_cents; updates payment.amount_refunded_cents.
Step 4: refund::ClawbackCoordinator::fanOut($refund) — dispatches RefundClawbackReferral (when materialized referral reward exists) + RefundClawbackCoupon (when redeemed coupon exists).
Step 5: refund::FireMarketingRefundIssued($refund) — dispatches marketing::RefundIssued negative-value conversion event.
Commit transaction
On any step failure: rollback + UPDATE refunds SET status='provider_error', failure_reason=? (surfaces to admin as 'finalization failed — retry via reconcile') + fire RefundProviderError
Emit stackra.refund.finalizations_total counter labelled by success/failure

jobs.purpose: Dispatched by RefundObserver.creating when requires_approval=true. Sends RefundApprovalRequiredNotification to every user holding `refunds.approve` permission for the tenant. Signed magic link in the notification deep-links to the approve/reject UI. Enforces segregation of duties — approver ≠ initiator (initiator's user_id is filtered out of the recipient list). Idempotent — refuses re-fire when refund is no longer in `awaiting_approval`.
steps: Load Refund row; refuse when status != 'awaiting_approval'

Resolve list of approvers: SELECT users.id FROM users JOIN role_user JOIN roles JOIN role_has_permissions JOIN permissions WHERE tenant_id=? AND permissions.name='refunds.approve' AND users.id != refund.initiated_by_user_id
For each approver: dispatch RefundApprovalRequiredNotification via NotificationsService
Record dispatched_approvers on refund.metadata.approval_notification (list of approver user_ids notified)
Emit stackra.refund.approvals_notified_total counter

jobs.purpose: Nightly. Reconciles every non-terminal refund against provider authoritative state. Dispatches CheckRefundProviderStatusJob per-provider for every refund still in status=processing + provider_error. Also detects orphaned refunds (provider says refund exists but we have no matching row) + reports as P2 signal. Alerts on drift > 0.
steps: Group refunds by provider: SELECT provider, count(*) FROM refunds WHERE status IN ('processing', 'provider_error') GROUP BY provider
For each provider: dispatch CheckRefundProviderStatusJob(provider=?)
Optional: per-provider audit — query the provider's list_refunds API for the last 7 days + cross-reference against our refunds table
For provider-side refunds without a local row: emit stackra.refund.orphaned_provider_refunds_total counter + P2 alert
For local refunds still in status=provider_error > 24h: emit stackra.refund.stale_provider_errors_total counter + P2 alert
Emit stackra.refund.reconciliation_run_duration_ms histogram

jobs.purpose: Weekly. Hard-purge refunds + refund_lines past 7-year (or 10-year Enterprise) retention. Invoice-linked refunds migrate to compliance_archive.refunds + compliance_archive.refund_lines prior to cascade (financial-obligation retention). Non-invoice-linked refunds hard-delete. Audit rows outlive per audit module design.
steps: Compute retention cutoff per tenant: coupon_extended_retention on = 10y; off = 7y (from processed_at)
For each tenant: SELECT refunds WHERE processed_at < cutoff AND deleted_at IS NULL FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 5000
For each refund: if invoice_id is set + parent invoice still exists in compliance_archive: INSERT INTO compliance_archive.refunds + compliance_archive.refund_lines
DELETE FROM refunds (FK CASCADE handles refund_lines)
Emit stackra.refund.retention_purged_total counter labelled by entity (refunds / refund_lines) + destination (hard_delete / compliance_archive)

jobs.purpose: Dispatches customer + admin notifications on refund state transitions. Called from RefundObserver.updating when status changes. Routes to the appropriate notification class based on the target state (RefundProcessedNotification for processing, RefundSucceededNotification for succeeded, RefundFailedNotification for failed, RefundStatusNotification for cancelled). Respects notification consent tier + tenant-configured cadence. Idempotent — each transition fires exactly one notification per recipient.
steps: Load Refund + parent Payment + Invoice + customer (customer_type + customer_id from parent invoice)
Look up recipients: customer (always, transactional consent), admin group (per tenant setting refund.notify_admins_on_status)

Dispatch appropriate notification class based on status: RefundRequestedNotification / RefundApprovedNotification / RefundProcessedNotification / RefundSucceededNotification / RefundFailedNotification / RefundRejectedNotification / RefundStatusNotification (cancelled)
Record dispatched_at on refund.metadata.notifications for idempotency
Emit stackra.refund.notifications_dispatched_total counter labelled by status + channel
